name: Andrés Duarte Ornelas
tagline: Cybersecurity graduate professional specializing in network security architecture, vulnerability assessment, and the intersection of LLMs with offensive and defensive security.
summary: Cybersecurity professional with hands-on experience in network security architecture, vulnerability assessment, incident response, and digital forensics. Currently pursuing dual master's degrees in Information Technology (Cybersecurity) at UTSA and Cybersecurity at ITESM. Passionate about network security, incident detection and response, and the emerging role of large language models in autonomous network operations.

experience.summary: Evaluated risks and implemented security solutions in production environments.
highlights: Evaluated risks in production environments and developed tailored mitigation solutions
Designed and implemented segmented office network using VLANs with DMZ for internet-facing services
Configured traffic rules to control inter-segment communication and enhance network security
Performed web vulnerability scanning using Zed Attack Proxy (ZAP) to identify and remediate threats
Assisted in creating and implementing company-wide cybersecurity policy framework
Gained hands-on experience in security incident management and response

experience.summary: Conducted vulnerability assessments and implemented system hardening strategies.
highlights: Conducted comprehensive vulnerability assessments and implemented system hardening strategies
Documented security findings and provided actionable recommendations to enhance cybersecurity posture

projects.name: Empirical Characterization of a Commercial Coding Agent for CLI-Based OSPF Fault Diagnosis
projects.description: Conducted the first empirical evaluation of a general-purpose coding agent (Claude Code) performing autonomous OSPF fault diagnosis in a traditional, non-SDN CLI environment, addressing a gap in existing LLM network operations research. Designed a 50-session quantitative experiment using a three-router GNS3/VyOS topology modeled after CCNA 11.6.3, with six simultaneously active faults per session and automated pre/post verification via Netmiko snapshots. Achieved an effective fault resolution rate of ~97% and a perfect-run rate of ~78% at a mean cost of $0.81/session; zero sessions introduced a configuration regression. Publicly released experimental materials, router configurations, and analysis scripts at github.com/Doarte45/LLMnetopsLab.

projects.name: Cloud as a Service Commercial and Operative Design
projects.description: Designed multi-cloud service architecture using AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud for company Quanti. Developed commercial strategy for service delivery and operational framework.

projects.name: Digital Forensic Investigation: "Posthumous Digital Note" Case Analysis
projects.description: Collaborated on comprehensive digital forensic analysis of suspicious death case using industry-standard tools. Analyzed disk images, RAM dumps, and Windows event logs to reconstruct timeline and establish evidence of third-party involvement. Documented complete chain of custody following ISO/IEC 27037 and NIST SP 800-86 standards.

education.degree: Master of Science in Information Technology, Cybersecurity Concentration
education.institution: University of Texas at San Antonio (UTSA)
education.detail: Currently enrolled in Spring 2026 semester taking UTSA electives. Part of UTSA-ITESM Dual Degree Program.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Electronics Engineering, Specialization in Cybersecurity

certifications.name: Cisco CyberOps Associate Certification
certifications.detail: Demonstrated comprehensive understanding of Security Operations including intrusion detection, analysis, and network monitoring. Completed 52 hands-on labs (30+ hours) using Security Onion, ELK Stack, and Cisco Packet Tracer.
